page: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-24xl is a compute-optimized bare-metal server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7i.metal-24xl demonstrates strong multi-core performance. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the server in the top tier and strong brackets. Its memory subsystem exhibits top-tier cached read bandwidth, though large-block write operations fall into the average tier. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark extended instructions and encryption tests, alongside strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates hypervisor overhead, maximizing the utility of the dedicated hardware. The 1 to 2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly resource-dense for compute-heavy workloads, though the absence of local storage requires external network-attached storage solutions. This server is suited for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, and multi-threaded database operations.
